GlobalPolicy.module-level-focus: Fast deterministic checks per module with trace assertions
GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Integration checks for merged surfaces touched by current wave only
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Full regression + traceability + fixture corpus validation
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log model API keys, user document content, or raw OOXML payloads exceeding 10KB. Edit-pipeline diff snippets (M-EDIT.render_diff: before_snippet, after_snippet, anchor.value) and extracted text fed to the LLM (M-EDIT.extract_text_with_anchors output) carry user content and are restricted to DEBUG log level only; INFO and above must reference them by op_id and length, never by literal text. Backup file paths may be logged at INFO; original document paths beyond the project workspace must be logged as basename only.

evidence-types.type-1: Exact assert on return value, state, or file content hash
evidence-types.type-2: Required log markers appeared in correct order; forbidden markers absent
evidence-types.type-3: End-to-end file produced + passes validation pipeline
evidence-types.type-4: Bounded evaluation against rubric (only where exact assert impossible)

TestFixtures.fixture-1: Well-formed DOCX passing all D-H rules
TestFixtures.fixture-5: Well-formed PPTX passing all P-H rules
TestFixtures.fixture-7: Minimal docx-js code producing valid DOCX
TestFixtures.fixture-8: Minimal pptxgenjs code producing valid PPTX
TestFixtures.fixture-9: Code with require('fs') for sandbox violation test
TestFixtures.fixture-10: Code with require('net') for sandbox violation test
TestFixtures.fixture-13: DOCX with GRACE Custom XML Parts already injected
TestFixtures.fixture-14: DOCX with non-GRACE Custom XML Parts for preservation test
TestFixtures.fixture-15: DOCX containing existing w:ins and w:del entries for accept_change / reject_change tests
TestFixtures.fixture-16: DOCX containing top-level comments in word/comments.xml plus matching commentRange markers in document.xml
TestFixtures.fixture-17: DOCX containing parent comment plus reply linked through word/commentsExtended.xml (w15:commentEx paraIdParent)
TestFixtures.fixture-18: DOCX containing ASCII straight quotes inside w:t for escape_smart_quotes round-trip tests
TestFixtures.fixture-19: DOCX containing w:durableId >= 0x7FFFFFFF for pack auto-repair test
TestFixtures.fixture-20: DOCX whose word/_rels/document.xml.rels references a missing Target for OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN test
TestFixtures.fixture-21: ~5MB DOCX, ~100 paragraphs distributed across ~100 pages, used for the M-EDIT non-functional latency budget (Wave-5-2 evidence-7). Generated procedurally by tests/fixtures/generate_large.py rather than checked in if > LFS threshold; see fixture-generation note below.
TestFixtures.fixture-note-1: fixture for V-M-EDIT scenario-24 (hash collision) is NOT pre-built on disk: the test stubs mint.edit._paragraph_hash via monkeypatch on a freshly-built two-paragraph fixture, so brute-forcing a real 8-hex SHA1 collision is unnecessary.
TestFixtures.fixture-note-2: fixture-21 (large_5mb) MAY be regenerated locally by the test runner (deterministic seed) to keep the repository under git-LFS thresholds; the generator script is the source of truth and is itself version-controlled.

VF-001.NAME: CodeGenerationPipeline
VF-001.scenario: Frontier/medium model writes JS code → sandbox executes → file produced
VF-001.expected-outcome: Valid DOCX/PPTX file passes L1 validation, no P0 errors

invariants.inv-1: Sandbox returns within 30s or raises SANDBOX_TIMEOUT
invariants.inv-2: Output file is well-formed OOXML (zip + XML parse)
invariants.inv-3: No forbidden patterns in executed code

VF-002.NAME: TemplateFillPipeline
VF-002.scenario: Small model provides JSON → template engine fills master → file produced
VF-002.expected-outcome: Valid DOCX/PPTX from template, design tokens applied, no P0 errors

invariants.inv-1: Template output is valid OOXML ZIP

VF-003.NAME: ValidateAndFix
VF-003.scenario: Document with violations → validation detects → auto-fix applies safe/visual fixes
VF-003.expected-outcome: Safe fixes silent, visual fixes with diff, destructive rejected with hint

invariants.inv-1: Backup file exists before any fix
invariants.inv-4: Cascade detected if violations not converging

VF-004.NAME: StyleExtraction
VF-004.expected-outcome: JSON contains colors, typography, layout; Layer 1 = 100% accurate

invariants.inv-1: Layer 1 output exactly matches theme/styles.xml content
invariants.inv-2: Output JSON is valid against design-tokens schema

VF-005.NAME: VisualQA
VF-005.expected-outcome: QA report with confidence scores; overlap >2% = violation

VF-006.NAME: GRACEInjection
VF-006.scenario: Document → GRACE manifest + instructions injected as Custom XML Parts
VF-006.expected-outcome: Metadata readable via OOXML parsers; existing Custom XML preserved

invariants.inv-2: Non-GRACE Custom XML Parts byte-identical after injection

VF-007.NAME: ModularGenerationPipeline
VF-007.scenario: Complex user prompt → M-PLAN decomposes into sections → M-SECTION generates code per section → M-ASSEMBLE builds complete document
VF-007.expected-outcome: DOCX with all planned sections, valid structure, passes L1 validation, no Word repair required

invariants.inv-1: Plan has at least 1 section with valid type and description
invariants.inv-2: Each section code has no import/require, no fs references, bracket balance OK
invariants.inv-3: Assembly JS is valid and produces output file within 30s sandbox timeout
invariants.inv-4: Output file is well-formed OOXML (ZIP + XML parse)
invariants.inv-5: Failed sections retried up to max_retries before overall failure
invariants.inv-6: Postprocessor applied: no empty comments.xml, tblLayout fixed, styles deduped

required-signals.trace-sequence: BLOCK_PLAN_DECOMPOSE -> BLOCK_PLAN_VALIDATE -> BLOCK_SECTION_GENERATE (per section) -> BLOCK_ASSEMBLE_TEMPLATE -> BLOCK_ASSEMBLE_EXECUTE -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED

forbidden-signals.forbidden-1: [Section] import or require in generated code

VF-008.NAME: PlanValidation
VF-008.scenario: LLM produces plan JSON → M-PLAN validates structure → rejects invalid plans with actionable error
VF-008.expected-outcome: Valid plans pass; invalid plans (empty sections, bad types, missing fields) rejected with clear error

invariants.inv-1: Plan has format matching request (docx or pptx)
invariants.inv-2: Each section has id, type (from enum), and description
invariants.inv-3: Style IDs are standard (Heading1-6, ListParagraph, Hyperlink)
invariants.inv-4: Page setup dimensions are valid DXA (width > 0, height > 0)

VF-009.NAME: SectionRetryRecovery
VF-009.scenario: Section generation fails (syntax error, truncation) → M-SECTION retries with refined prompt → succeeds on retry. If all retries exhausted → placeholder generated, assembly proceeds.
VF-009.expected-outcome: Individual section failure produces placeholder (title + italic error message). Assembly includes placeholders. Overall result success=False but document still produced.

invariants.inv-2: Retry prompt includes: original section spec + previous error message + previous failed code as negative example
invariants.inv-3: Global retry budget enforced: max_total_retries (default 20) across all sections
invariants.inv-4: If all retries exhausted for a section, placeholder code generated (not empty, not skipped)
invariants.inv-5: Assembly includes all sections — failed ones have placeholder children
invariants.inv-6: CreateResult.success=False if any section used placeholder, sections_succeeded < sections_total

VF-010.NAME: DocumentEditPipeline
VF-010.scenario: EditPlan JSON (typed ops) → M-EDIT validates plan → M-OOXML unpacks → M-EDIT resolves anchors against original tree → applies typed handlers in order → M-OOXML packs → M-VALIDATE checks output
VF-010.expected-outcome: Output passes M-VALIDATE in the configured severity mode; backup of the original is preserved on disk; per-op diff is returned with before/after snippets; non-edited binary parts and unrelated XML parts round-trip unchanged; the LLM (when used to produce the plan) never sees raw OOXML XML

invariants.inv-1: Every op_id in the plan is unique
invariants.inv-2: Every anchor.value is non-empty, length ≤ 512, no ASCII control characters
invariants.inv-3: Backup file is created before any tree mutation
invariants.inv-4: Anchors of every type (paragraph_index, hash, text) resolve against the ORIGINAL tree captured at unpack; no re-indexing across ops
invariants.inv-5: Tracked-change ops emit ECMA-376 w:ins/w:del with author and date; w:rPr is preserved on both branches
invariants.inv-6: Comment replies are linked through word/commentsExtended.xml (w15:commentEx paraIdParent), never by nesting commentRange in document.xml
invariants.inv-7: commentRangeStart/End markers are siblings of w:r, never nested inside w:r
invariants.inv-8: build_edit_prompt does not include any raw OOXML XML in the LLM-facing prompt
invariants.inv-9: Output zip preserves [Content_Types].xml override entry order and binary parts byte-for-byte for unmodified content
invariants.inv-10: EditMetadata.source_prompt_hash is computed by M-EDIT, never trusted from the model output

required-signals.conditional-marker: [OOXML][autorepair][BLOCK_OOXML_AUTOREPAIR] — fires inside BLOCK_OOXML_PACK only when the input requires repair (durableId overflow, missing xml:space=preserve, malformed rsid). Optional in trace.

[Edit][tracked_change][BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE] — fires inside BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP only for ops with type in {tracked_replace, tracked_delete, accept_change, reject_change}. Optional in trace; required when at least one such op is in the plan.
required-signals.trace-sequence: BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE -> BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP -> BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK -> BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT -> BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR(per op) -> BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP(per op, may emit BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE) -> BLOCK_OOXML_PACK(may emit BLOCK_OOXML_AUTOREPAIR) -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED

forbidden-signals.forbidden-1: Any opening OOXML element tag (regex /<[a-z][a-z0-9]*:[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]*(\s|>|\/)/ matching any of the OOXML namespaces w:, w14:, w15:, w16:, wp:, wps:, a:, m:, mc:, r:, pic:) inside the LLM prompt produced by build_edit_prompt — checked by unit test on rendered prompt string
forbidden-signals.forbidden-2: BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP fires before BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE has emitted a success record
forbidden-signals.forbidden-3: BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK fires before BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP has emitted a success record
forbidden-signals.forbidden-4: Output written without a preceding BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS

scenarios.scenario-1: loadConfig reads LLM_BASE_URL + LLM_MODEL + MINT_MODEL_TIER from env → valid MintConfig
scenarios.scenario-2: loadConfig reads .env file when env vars absent → valid MintConfig
scenarios.scenario-3: Missing LLM_BASE_URL raises CONFIG_MISSING with message containing "LLM_BASE_URL"
scenarios.scenario-4: Invalid MINT_MODEL_TIER (e.g. "huge") raises CONFIG_INVALID with valid options listed

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Never returns None — must raise or return valid MintConfig
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Never logs API keys at INFO level

V-M-CONFIG.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — no external deps, pure config loading, fully deterministic

scenarios.scenario-1: execute(hello_world_docx.js) → SandboxResult(success=True, output_path exists, duration_ms > 0)
scenarios.scenario-2: execute(hello_world_pptx.js) → SandboxResult(success=True, output_path exists)
scenarios.scenario-3: execute(malicious_fs.js) → raises SANDBOX_VIOLATION_FS with "fs" in message
scenarios.scenario-4: execute(malicious_net.js) → raises SANDBOX_VIOLATION_NET with "net" in message
scenarios.scenario-5: execute(infinite_loop.js) → SANDBOX_TIMEOUT within 30s + 2s tolerance, BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE logged
scenarios.scenario-6: execute(syntax_error.js) → SANDBOX_RUNTIME_ERROR with stderr containing error line

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Code with require('fs'), require('net'), require('child_process'), require('http'), require('https') must be rejected in validate_code BEFORE execution
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: process.exit() in user code must be rejected
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Never write files outside designated output directory

V-M-SANDBOX.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — security-critical, requires manual review of sandbox isolation before autonomous execution

scenarios.scenario-1: D-H01 rule: bad_column_widths.docx → Violation(rule_id="D-H01", severity="hard")
scenarios.scenario-2: D-H03 rule: percentage_width.docx → Violation(rule_id="D-H03", severity="hard", fix_category="destructive")
scenarios.scenario-3: D-H09 rule: raw_newline.docx → Violation(rule_id="D-H09")
scenarios.scenario-4: D-H01..D-H10 rules: minimal_valid.docx → zero violations (0 false positives)
scenarios.scenario-5: D-S01..D-S05 soft rules classify as fix_category safe or visual
scenarios.scenario-6: P-H01..P-H05 rules detect violations in crafted bad PPTX fixtures
scenarios.scenario-7: get_hint("D-H03") → dict with keys: rule, reason, fix_instruction

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: evaluate must never modify the input document
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Rule loading must fail fast on invalid YAML schema

V-M-RULES.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — pure functions, no side effects, fully deterministic

scenarios.scenario-1: run_checks(minimal_valid.docx, audit) → ValidationReport(passed=True, violations=[], mode="audit")
scenarios.scenario-2: run_checks(minimal_valid.docx, lenient) → ValidationReport(passed=True)
scenarios.scenario-3: run_checks(bad_column_widths.docx, audit) → ValidationReport(passed=True, hard_count=1) — audit logs but does not reject
scenarios.scenario-4: run_checks(bad_column_widths.docx, lenient) → ValidationReport(passed=False, hard_count=1)
scenarios.scenario-5: run_checks(docx_with_soft_violation, strict) → ValidationReport(passed=False) — strict rejects on warnings
scenarios.scenario-7: BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS logged with violation count and mode

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never modify the input document

scenarios.scenario-1: apply_fixes(docx_with_safe_violation) → FixReport(applied_fixes > 0, backup_path exists, iterations=1)
scenarios.scenario-2: apply_fixes(docx_with_visual_violation) → FixReport with non-empty diff field
scenarios.scenario-3: apply_fixes(docx_with_destructive_violation) → raises DESTRUCTIVE_REJECTED with educational hint
scenarios.scenario-4: apply_fixes(docx_with_cascading_violation, max_iterations=3) → raises CASCADE_DETECTED after 3 iterations

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never fix without creating backup first
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must stop after 3 iterations even if violations remain

V-M-FIX.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — modifies files, but backup + max iterations + destructive rejection provide safety

scenarios.scenario-1: compute(minimal_valid.docx) twice → identical hex hash
scenarios.scenario-2: compute(bad_column_widths.docx) != compute(minimal_valid.docx) — different styles.xml
scenarios.scenario-3: compare(hash1, hash1) → DriftStatus.MATCH
scenarios.scenario-4: compare(hash1, hash2) → DriftStatus.DRIFT

V-M-FINGERPRINT.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — pure computation, no side effects, fully deterministic

scenarios.scenario-1: mint_validate(minimal_valid.docx) → JSON with passed=True, violations=[]
scenarios.scenario-2: mint_validate(bad_column_widths.docx) → JSON with passed=False, violations[0].rule_id="D-H01"
scenarios.scenario-3: mint_fix(docx_with_safe_violation) → JSON with applied_fixes, diff, backup_path
scenarios.scenario-4: mint_fingerprint(minimal_valid.docx) → JSON with hash (64-char hex) and drift_status

V-M-MCP-G1.wave-follow-up: Verify MCP-G1 works with MCP-G2 mounted (no tool name collisions)
V-M-MCP-G1.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — thin adapter over tested modules

scenarios.scenario-1: select_skill(frontier, docx) → file path matching "*frontier*docx*"
scenarios.scenario-2: select_skill(small, pptx) → file path matching "*small*pptx*"
scenarios.scenario-3: select_skill(frontier, xlsx) → raises SKILL_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-4: render_prompt(skill, design_tokens={...}) → prompt string containing "colors" and "primary"

scenarios.scenario-1: fill(board_deck.pptx, template_content.json) → output.pptx that is valid ZIP with replaced content
scenarios.scenario-2: fill(board_deck.pptx, invalid_json) → raises JSON_SCHEMA_ERROR with field path
scenarios.scenario-3: fill with design_tokens → output font/color matches token values (XPath assertion)
scenarios.scenario-4: list_templates() → list containing at least "board-deck" and "business-memo"

V-M-TEMPLATES.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — file manipulation with validation, deterministic

scenarios.scenario-1: extract_style(docx_with_known_theme) → DesignTokens.colors.primary == "#E6007E" (exact match to known fixture)
scenarios.scenario-2: extract_style(docx_with_known_theme) → DesignTokens.typography.heading.font == "Arial"
scenarios.scenario-3: extract_style(complex_pptx) → detected_layouts list with at least 1 entry (statistical, ~80%)
scenarios.scenario-4: extract_style(non_ooxml_file) → raises EXTRACTION_FAILED

scenarios.scenario-1: create(CreateRequest with model_response_override=hello_world_docx.js, tier=frontier) → CreateResult(success=True, execution_mode="code")
scenarios.scenario-2: create(CreateRequest with template_content.json, tier=small, template_name="board-deck") → CreateResult(success=True, execution_mode="template")
scenarios.scenario-3: create with sandbox violation code → CreateResult(success=False, error contains "SANDBOX")
scenarios.scenario-4: BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE → BLOCK_SELECT_SKILL → (BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE or BLOCK_FILL_TEMPLATE) → BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS all logged

V-M-CREATE.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — orchestrates multiple modules, depends on external model API

scenarios.scenario-1: mint_create with code mode → file + validation report
scenarios.scenario-2: mint_extract_style(docx_fixture) → JSON with colors.typography.layout keys
scenarios.scenario-3: mint_list_templates() → non-empty list with name + format fields
scenarios.scenario-4: mint_edit(document_path=fixture, edit_plan_json=valid plan with 1 replace_text op, author='Tester') → EditResult JSON with success=True, ops_total=1, ops_succeeded=1, output_path set, backup_path set; output validates clean
scenarios.scenario-5: mint_edit(edit_plan_json=malformed JSON) returns MCP_TOOL_ERROR with parse error message; no file mutation
scenarios.scenario-6: mint_edit(edit_plan_json with format='pptx') returns EditResult with success=False, error='EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED'; backup still created? No — Phase-5 oos-2 contract: PPTX rejected before backup
scenarios.scenario-7: mint_edit invocation emits the full VF-010 trace-sequence (BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE → BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP → BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK → BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT → BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR → BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP → BLOCK_OOXML_PACK → BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS) — markers captured by the integration test harness
scenarios.scenario-8: mint_edit returned EditResult JSON is byte-identical to direct M-EDIT.edit(...) → asdict(result) for the same inputs (same document, same plan, same author) — guards against MCP-layer drift

V-M-MCP-G2.wave-follow-up: Full pipeline test: extract_style → create with tokens → validate → QA. Phase-5 follow-up: mint_edit happy path on each fixture from 15-20.
V-M-MCP-G2.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — thin adapter over tested modules

scenarios.scenario-1: run_l1(minimal_valid.docx) → L1Report with passed=True, duration_ms < 1000
scenarios.scenario-2: run_l1(bad_column_widths.docx) → L1Report with passed=False, violations non-empty
scenarios.scenario-3: run_l2(docx_fixture) with Gotenberg mock → L2Report with confidence score 0.0-1.0
scenarios.scenario-4: run_l2(docx_fixture) with Gotenberg unavailable → raises GOTENBERG_UNAVAILABLE

V-M-QA.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — L1 is deterministic; L2 depends on Gotenberg availability

scenarios.scenario-1: bootstrap(minimal_valid.docx) → output.docx with urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest in Custom XML Parts
scenarios.scenario-2: bootstrap output: manifest contains document_structure + fingerprint fields
scenarios.scenario-3: bootstrap(with_vendor_xml.docx): non-GRACE XML Parts byte-identical before/after (hash comparison)
scenarios.scenario-4: describe(with_grace.docx) → GRACEManifest with instructions list length 5-10

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must not remove or alter existing Custom XML Parts from other namespaces

scenarios.scenario-1: validate_plan(plan with 3 sections, valid types, heading styles) → passes without errors
scenarios.scenario-2: validate_plan(plan with empty sections list) → raises PLAN_EMPTY
scenarios.scenario-3: validate_plan(plan with section type="unknown") → raises PLAN_INVALID with "unknown type"
scenarios.scenario-4: validate_plan(plan with standard page setup 12240x15840) → passes
scenarios.scenario-5: validate_plan(plan with page width=0) → raises PLAN_INVALID

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never return a plan with duplicate section IDs
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never accept non-standard style IDs (e.g. "MyHeading")

V-M-PLAN.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — depends on external LLM, but output is validated JSON

scenarios.scenario-1: validate_section_code(code with valid Paragraph/TextRun patterns) → passes
scenarios.scenario-2: validate_section_code(code with import 'docx') → raises SECTION_CODE_INVALID
scenarios.scenario-3: validate_section_code(code with unmatched brackets) → raises SECTION_CODE_INVALID with "bracket"
scenarios.scenario-4: render_section_prompt(plan, section_id="s1") → prompt with section title, description, sibling context
scenarios.scenario-5: generate_all with mock LLM returning valid code → all SectionCode(success=True)
scenarios.scenario-6: generate_all with 1 section failing then succeeding on retry → final all success

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never pass code with require() or import to assembler
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never exceed max_retries per section

V-M-SECTION.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — depends on external LLM per section, retry logic provides resilience

scenarios.scenario-1: build_styles_config(plan with Heading1/2/3) → JS string containing "Heading1", "Heading2", "Heading3" with correct sizes and colors
scenarios.scenario-2: build_numbering_config(plan with bullets + decimal) → JS string with LevelFormat.BULLET and LevelFormat.DECIMAL
scenarios.scenario-3: build_headers_footers(plan with header="Title", page_numbers=True) → JS string with PositionalTab and PageNumber.CURRENT
scenarios.scenario-4: assemble(plan with 2 sections, mock code) → sandbox executes → valid DOCX file
scenarios.scenario-5: assemble(plan, sections missing required section_id) → raises ASSEMBLY_MISSING_SECTIONS
scenarios.scenario-6: Full assembly output → postprocessor fixes applied (no empty comments, tblLayout fixed)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never call LLM — assembly is purely deterministic
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must not produce JS with syntax errors — template must be pre-validated

V-M-ASSEMBLE.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — pure code generation + sandbox execution, fully deterministic given valid inputs

scenarios.scenario-1: LLMClient.call(prompt="hello") with a stubbed httpx transport returning {choices:[{message:{content:"world"}}]} produces LLMResponse(text="world", model=..., usage=..., duration_ms>=0)
scenarios.scenario-2: httpx transport returning HTTP 500 raises LLMCallError with status_code=500 and the error body in the message
scenarios.scenario-3: httpx transport raising httpx.TimeoutException raises LLM_TIMEOUT (or LLMCallError carrying timeout context)
scenarios.scenario-4: LLMClient never logs api_key or full prompt at INFO; redaction policy applies (api_key never logged at any level; prompt and response text only at DEBUG)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never log api_key, full prompt, or full response at INFO or above
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never silently swallow non-2xx responses

V-M-LLM.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — thin httpx wrapper with deterministic stub coverage

scenarios.scenario-1: mint validate fixture.docx --severity=lenient prints ValidationReport JSON to stdout, exit 0 on pass / non-zero on fail
scenarios.scenario-2: mint fingerprint fixture.docx prints FingerprintResult JSON, exit 0
scenarios.scenario-3: mint create docx "prompt" --tier=frontier --model-response-file fixture.js prints CreateResult JSON; output file exists at the path inside the JSON
scenarios.scenario-4: mint extract fixture.docx prints DesignTokens JSON
scenarios.scenario-5: mint create with unknown subcommand or missing required arg exits CLI_USAGE_ERROR (non-zero) and prints argparse-style usage to stderr
scenarios.scenario-6: mint validate /nonexistent/path.docx exits CLI_FILE_NOT_FOUND (non-zero) with a clear error line on stderr
scenarios.scenario-7: (Phase-5) mint edit fixture.docx --plan plan.json prints EditResult JSON; output_path inside the JSON points to an existing file

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never crash with an unhandled exception leaking traceback to stdout (stderr is acceptable for genuine bugs)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never print non-JSON content to stdout for the JSON-emitting subcommands

V-M-CLI.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — thin dispatcher; integration tests cover end-to-end behavior

scenarios.scenario-1: unpack(minimal_valid_docx) → returns parts list including [Content_Types].xml, word/document.xml, word/styles.xml; all XML files parse without errors
scenarios.scenario-2: unpack → pack → unpack round-trip on minimal_valid_docx: part listing identical, every XML part tree-equal, every binary part byte-equal
scenarios.scenario-3: merge_runs collapses two adjacent w:r with identical w:rPr into one; never merges across w:hyperlink, w:bookmarkStart, w:bookmarkEnd, w:commentRangeStart, w:commentRangeEnd, w:commentReference, w:ins, w:del, w:fldChar, w:tab, w:br, w:sym, or any element introducing a non-text child between the runs
scenarios.scenario-4: escape_smart_quotes converts ASCII apostrophes to ’ in body text but skips runs with rStyle ending in 'Code'; idempotent on already-escaped input
scenarios.scenario-4b: escape_smart_quotes alternates double quotes per paragraph: first ASCII '"' → “, second → ”, third → “, etc. Unpaired (odd) double quote in a paragraph is left as ASCII '"'. Reset across paragraphs. Mixed apostrophes and double quotes counted independently.
scenarios.scenario-5: pack auto-repair: input with w:durableId=0xFFFFFFFE produces output with regenerated valid id; preserved_whitespace_runs increments for each w:t with leading/trailing whitespace lacking xml:space=preserve
scenarios.scenario-8: validate_relationships on unpack tree with dangling Target raises OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN naming the missing target
scenarios.scenario-9: [Content_Types].xml Override entry order in pack output equals input order exactly

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never invoke LibreOffice, soffice, or any external converter
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never silently drop or rename a part during round-trip
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must never double-escape an existing XML entity

V-M-OOXML.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — pure stdlib zipfile + lxml manipulation, deterministic, no LLM

scenarios.scenario-1: validate_plan(EditPlan with 1 replace_text op + valid anchor) passes
scenarios.scenario-2: validate_plan(EditPlan with empty ops) raises EDIT_PLAN_INVALID
scenarios.scenario-3: validate_plan(op with anchor.value containing ASCII control characters or exceeding 512 chars) raises EDIT_PLAN_INVALID
scenarios.scenario-4: edit(minimal_valid_docx, replace_text "Hello" → "Hi") produces output passing M-VALIDATE; before_snippet/after_snippet captured in diff
scenarios.scenario-5: resolve_anchor on text appearing twice without context_before/after raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS
scenarios.scenario-6: resolve_anchor on text not present raises EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-7: edit with insert_paragraph(style="Heading2") after first paragraph: output has new w:p with w:pStyle w:val="Heading2", validates clean
scenarios.scenario-8: tracked_replace op produces sibling w:del + w:ins with author/date attributes; w:rPr preserved on both branches
scenarios.scenario-9: tracked_delete on full paragraph also marks paragraph mark via w:pPr/w:rPr/w:del
scenarios.scenario-10: add_comment with parent_id=0 creates a top-level commentRangeStart/End pair around the target plus a sibling commentRangeStart/End pair for the reply; the reply linkage lives in word/commentsExtended.xml as w15:commentEx with paraIdParent matching the parent paraId; comments.xml carries both top-level and reply text; content types registered
scenarios.scenario-11: add_comment respects "commentRangeStart/End are siblings of w:r, never inside w:r" invariant — schema check on document.xml confirms
scenarios.scenario-12: accept_change on an insertion by id removes the w:ins wrapper and keeps inserted text; accept_change on a deletion by id removes the w:del subtree (deleted text is gone); reject_change on an insertion drops the w:ins subtree (inserted text gone); reject_change on a deletion removes the w:del wrapper and restores w:t from w:delText
scenarios.scenario-13: build_edit_prompt does not include any raw OOXML XML in the LLM-facing prompt — only extracted text + stable anchors + op schema
scenarios.scenario-14: execute_op on an op carrying an unsupported type (for example type="rotate_image" in v0) raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before any tree mutation
scenarios.scenario-15: edit run end-to-end: backup created, M-VALIDATE invoked on output, EditResult.success=True, diff.length == ops_total
scenarios.scenario-16: set_paragraph_style with style_id="Heading2" on body paragraph: output paragraph has w:pStyle w:val="Heading2", original w:rPr preserved on existing runs, M-VALIDATE clean
scenarios.scenario-17: set_paragraph_style with style_id missing from styles.xml raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before mutation
scenarios.scenario-18: delete_paragraph on anchored paragraph removes the w:p subtree; relationships referenced only from that paragraph are removed from document.xml.rels; M-VALIDATE clean
scenarios.scenario-19: paragraph_index anchors resolve against the original tree even after earlier ops in the same plan inserted or deleted paragraphs (no re-indexing)
scenarios.scenario-20: EditMetadata.source_prompt_hash is computed by M-EDIT (not read from model output) and is stable for identical inputs
scenarios.scenario-21: EditPlan with op-1 delete_paragraph(anchor=A) followed by op-2 anchored at A by paragraph_index/hash/text: op-2 raises EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND because the original-tree anchor for A no longer resolves after op-1 mutated the tree, surfacing the documented "no re-indexing across ops" semantics
scenarios.scenario-22: edit(severity_mode="strict") on a fixture that produces a soft violation in the output triggers EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED and refuses to write output_path; backup remains intact
scenarios.scenario-23: EditPlan JSON uses 'type' as the discriminator field on both EditOp and Anchor (not 'op_type' and not 'kind') — schema test asserts no other discriminator name appears in the schema
scenarios.scenario-24: resolve_anchor with type='hash' on a tree where two different paragraphs share the same 8-hex SHA1 prefix raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS; the runtime never silently picks the first match. Test mechanics: a real 8-hex SHA1 collision is impractical to brute-force, so the test monkeypatches mint.edit._paragraph_hash (or equivalent helper) to return a fixed value 'deadbeef' for two distinct paragraphs in a fresh fixture; the assertion is that resolve_anchor raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS with both paragraph_index values listed in the error message.
scenarios.scenario-25: edit when the backup destination is unwritable (read-only directory or permission denied) raises BACKUP_FAILED before any unpack or tree mutation; the original document is unchanged on disk
scenarios.scenario-26: edit with output_path omitted writes to '<input-stem>.edited<ext>' next to the input; backup is at '<input>.bak'; output_path != backup_path
scenarios.scenario-27: edit on an EditPlan with format='pptx' raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before any tree mutation (PPTX edits are deferred to v0.5)
scenarios.scenario-28: successful edit emits BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP exactly once and strictly before BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK; the backup file exists on disk with identical bytes to the original input
scenarios.scenario-29: successful edit emits BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT exactly once and strictly before the first BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR; the rendered prompt for an LLM-driven plan is stable for identical input + anchor list

required-log-markers.marker-7: [Edit][tracked_change][BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE] (conditional — only when at least one op type is in {tracked_replace, tracked_delete, accept_change, reject_change})
required-log-markers.trace-sequence: BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE -> BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP -> BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK -> BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT -> BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR(per op) -> BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP(per op, may emit BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE) -> BLOCK_OOXML_PACK -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never expose raw OOXML XML to the LLM in build_edit_prompt
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never apply operations if validate_plan failed
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must never write output if M-VALIDATE on the result fails in strict mode without surfacing EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Must never modify tracked-change entries authored by other users beyond the documented per-id accept_change/reject_change ops
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: Must never proceed when anchor resolution is ambiguous; must raise EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS

V-M-EDIT.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — depends on external LLM for plan generation, but plan is typed JSON and every op is validated and bounded; backup is mandatory before any tree mutation

Wave-1-1.goal: Foundation modules: config loads, sandbox executes valid code and blocks invalid

required-evidence.evidence-2: Sandbox produces valid DOCX from hello_world fixture
required-evidence.evidence-3: Sandbox blocks fs and net access

Wave-1-2.goal: Full validation pipeline: rules → validate → fix → fingerprint

required-evidence.evidence-1: All D-H01..D-H10 rules detect violations in crafted fixtures
required-evidence.evidence-2: Validation report correctly classified by severity mode
required-evidence.evidence-3: Safe fixes applied, destructive rejected, cascade detected

Wave-1-3.goal: MCP G1 tools integration: validate + fix + fingerprint via MCP protocol

Wave-2-1.goal: Skill prompt selection + template engine basic fill

Wave-2-2.goal: Full generation pipeline: extract → create → validate → MCP tools

Wave-3-1.goal: QA pipeline (L1 programmatic + L2 render via Gotenberg) and GRACE manifest injection ship together; existing Custom XML Parts from other vendors are preserved.

required-evidence.evidence-1: L1 programmatic check on minimal_valid_docx and minimal_valid_pptx returns confidence ≥ 0.95 within 1s; trace contains [QA][l1_check][BLOCK_L1_CHECK]
required-evidence.evidence-2: L2 render path on minimal_valid_docx invokes Gotenberg, writes a PNG snapshot, runs overlap/margin checks; trace contains [QA][l2_render][BLOCK_L2_RENDER]; if Gotenberg is unreachable the test marks itself xfail rather than failing the wave
required-evidence.evidence-3: GRACE bootstrap on docx_with_vendor_xml fixture preserves the non-GRACE Custom XML Parts byte-for-byte; trace contains [GRACE][inject][BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST] and [GRACE][preserve][BLOCK_PRESERVE_EXISTING]
required-evidence.evidence-4: GRACE manifest namespace urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest is present in the output and readable by a fresh OOXML parser

Wave-3-1.regression-confirmed: Empirically validated during the Phase-5 plan-audit run: full pytest suite (191 tests) passes, ruff clean, mypy clean. Commands and evidence-blocks reflect actual test behavior on the merged tree.

Wave-4-1.goal: Document planner: plan generation + plan validation

required-evidence.evidence-1: Plan validation accepts valid plans and rejects invalid ones
required-evidence.evidence-2: Plan prompt includes format-specific constraints (DXA, style IDs)
required-evidence.evidence-3: Plan generation produces structured JSON with sections, styles, numbering

Wave-4-2.goal: Section generator: per-section code generation + validation + retry

required-evidence.evidence-1: Section code validation catches imports, bracket errors, missing patterns
required-evidence.evidence-2: Section prompt includes plan context + sibling summaries
required-evidence.evidence-3: Retry logic handles failures and succeeds on retry

Wave-4-3.goal: Document assembler: template rendering + sandbox execution

required-evidence.evidence-1: Styles config renders correct JS for Heading1-6 with colors/sizes
required-evidence.evidence-2: Numbering config renders bullet + decimal levels
required-evidence.evidence-3: Full assembly produces valid DOCX passing L1 validation
required-evidence.evidence-4: Postprocessor applied: no empty comments, tblLayout fixed

Wave-4-4.goal: Full modular pipeline integration: plan → sections → assemble → validate

required-evidence.evidence-1: Modular create produces multi-section document with >10 paragraphs
required-evidence.evidence-2: MCP tools mint_plan, mint_generate_section, mint_assemble work independently
required-evidence.evidence-3: All VF-007 trace markers logged in correct sequence

Wave-5-1.goal: Pure-Python unpack/pack round-trip is tree-stable for XML parts and byte-stable for binary parts; auto-repair fires on the documented inputs.

required-evidence.evidence-1: unpack → pack → unpack round-trip on minimal_valid_docx, docx_with_grace, docx_with_vendor_xml: every XML part tree-equal, every binary part byte-equal, [Content_Types].xml override order preserved
required-evidence.evidence-2: Auto-repair on docx_with_durable_id_overflow regenerates the durableId; repaired_durable_ids ≥ 1 in PackResult
required-evidence.evidence-3: validate_relationships on docx_with_dangling_rel raises OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN with the missing target named in the message
required-evidence.evidence-4: escape_smart_quotes on docx_with_quote_chars is idempotent: applying twice produces the same tree as applying once
required-evidence.evidence-5: No call site invokes LibreOffice / soffice / pandoc / any external binary. Static check: src/mint/ooxml.py imports only from {zipfile, lxml.etree, hashlib, pathlib, dataclasses, typing, logging, re, unicodedata}; the regex test asserts no occurrence of subprocess, os.system, os.popen, os.execv, os.execvp, pty.spawn, sh., or any 'shell=True' string literal. Positive framing: the only allowed external interaction is stdlib zipfile + lxml.

Wave-5-2.goal: Typed EditPlans applied deterministically; LLM never sees OOXML; revisions and comments follow ECMA-376 with reply linkage in commentsExtended.xml.

required-evidence.evidence-1: build_edit_prompt output contains no raw OOXML XML — assertion against the rendered prompt string using regex /<[a-z][a-z0-9]*:[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]*(\s|>|\/)/ which catches any opening tag with an XML namespace prefix (w:, w14:, w15:, w16:, wp:, wps:, a:, m:, mc:, r:, pic: and any future OOXML namespace). The same regex is the executable form of VF-010.forbidden-1.
required-evidence.evidence-2: tracked_replace + tracked_delete produce ECMA-376 w:ins / w:del with author and date; w:rPr preserved on both branches; full-paragraph delete also marks w:pPr/w:rPr/w:del
required-evidence.evidence-3: add_comment with parent_id=0 produces both top-level and reply entries in word/comments.xml plus a w15:commentEx paraIdParent record in word/commentsExtended.xml; commentRange markers are siblings of w:r
required-evidence.evidence-4: accept_change and reject_change behave per matrix in V-M-EDIT scenario-12 (insertion vs deletion)
required-evidence.evidence-5: Anchor resolution against ORIGINAL tree confirmed by VF-010.inv-4 + V-M-EDIT scenario-19 + scenario-21
required-evidence.evidence-6: EditPlan JSON uses 'type' as the discriminator on both EditOp and Anchor (V-M-EDIT scenario-23)
required-evidence.evidence-7: M-EDIT.edit on fixture-21 (docx_large_5mb, ~5MB, ~100 pages, ~100 paragraphs) with a 3-op EditPlan completes within p99 ≤ 5s wall-clock on the reference machine (CI runner spec, single-process, warm filesystem). Implementation note for the worker: M-OOXML.unpack/pack must avoid full reparse-on-every-part churn; M-EDIT must avoid O(n²) anchor scans. Latency budget is per-call, excluding LLM round-trip. Track regressions via pytest --benchmark or equivalent.

Wave-5-3.goal: End-to-end edit through the MCP tool and the CLI subcommand: EditPlan JSON in, EditResult JSON out, output document validated.

required-evidence.evidence-1: mint_edit MCP tool accepts edit_plan_json + author and returns EditResult JSON identical to direct M-EDIT.edit invocation
required-evidence.evidence-2: mint edit CLI subcommand reads EditPlan from a JSON file path and exits non-zero when EditResult.success is false
required-evidence.evidence-3: VF-010 trace-sequence emitted in correct order on a successful edit through the MCP path

Gate-Phase-1.goal: G0 hypothesis validated + G1 deterministic quality gate operational

required-evidence.evidence-1: Sandbox executes docx-js code without violations
required-evidence.evidence-2: All D-H01..D-H10 and P-H01..P-H05 rules have tests with fixtures
required-evidence.evidence-4: StyleFingerprint hash is deterministic for same input
required-evidence.evidence-5: 0 false negatives, <5% false positives on test fixture corpus
required-evidence.evidence-6: All trace markers logged for VF-003 critical flow

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Any model + MINT ≈ Claude document quality (core promise)

required-evidence.evidence-1: Skill prompts auto-selected by tier and format
required-evidence.evidence-2: Template engine fills JSON → valid DOCX/PPTX
required-evidence.evidence-3: Style extraction produces design-tokens.json matching fixture
required-evidence.evidence-4: CreateResult.execution_mode correctly set based on tier
required-evidence.evidence-5: All trace markers logged for VF-001 and VF-002 critical flows

Gate-Phase-3.goal: Visual QA + GRACE metadata + public launch readiness

required-evidence.evidence-1: QA L2 detects overlap >2% as violation
required-evidence.evidence-2: GRACE metadata readable from injected documents
required-evidence.evidence-3: Cross-model edit test passes (GLM creates → Qwen edits → MINT validates)
required-evidence.evidence-4: All trace markers logged for VF-004, VF-005, VF-006

Gate-Phase-4.goal: Modular generation achieves 90%+ success on 35B models for complex multi-section documents

required-evidence.evidence-1: Plan validation accepts valid plans, rejects invalid (all V-M-PLAN scenarios pass)
required-evidence.evidence-2: Section code generation produces valid JS per section (all V-M-SECTION scenarios pass)
required-evidence.evidence-3: Assembly produces valid DOCX passing L1 validation (all V-M-ASSEMBLE scenarios pass)
required-evidence.evidence-4: Showcase document generated via modular pipeline has >10 paragraphs, >1 table, headings, lists, callouts
required-evidence.evidence-5: Showcase document opens in Word without repair dialog
required-evidence.evidence-6: All trace markers logged for VF-007, VF-008, VF-009 critical flows

packet-example.observed-evidence: Fix loop ran 4 iterations without raising
packet-example.next-action: Check iteration counting logic in M-FIX apply_fixes
